ogTitle: Smoky Creek vs Somerset: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Smoky Creek and Somerset.
heading: Smoky Creek vs Somerset — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Smoky Creek, Queensland and Somerset, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Smoky Creek and Somerset have similar overall climate risk scores.

faq.question: Is Smoky Creek or Somerset safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Smoky Creek has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Somerset scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Smoky Creek scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Somerset scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Smoky Creek or Somerset?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Smoky Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area face minimal climate value impact based on current projections.
